semantic.role: Repair policy decision engine for the Spine gate output mutation check. Determines whether to perform targeted repair, prompt a full rebuild, or require a full rebuild.
responsibilities: Evaluate a ProtectedOutputMutationReport and produce a RepairPolicyDecision.
Classify each changed/added/removed path as a source file, aggregate-level path, or unmapped path.
Determine if aggregate-level paths are sufficiently covered by targeted source files.
Compute the appropriate repair action (targeted-repair, prompt-full-rebuild, require-full-rebuild).
Provide a safe non-interactive downgrade option when conditions allow.
outOfScope: Executing the repair or any file mutations.
Interacting with LLMs or other external services.
Persisting the decision or managing state across sessions.
Handling errors or recovery from failed repairs.
invariants: The evaluateRepairPolicy function always returns a valid RepairPolicyDecision object.
The decision is deterministic given the same input report.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Provides a policy layer for deciding how to respond to output mutations in the Spine gate, enabling safe automated repair decisions.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: Recent commit 'ArchSpine v1.0.0 — Architectural Governance & Semantic Layer for the AI Era' indicates this is part of the initial stable release of the repair policy engine with the core governance capabilities.

publicSurface.description: Evaluates a ProtectedOutputMutationReport and returns a RepairPolicyDecision indicating the repair action (targeted-repair, prompt-full-rebuild, or require-full-rebuild), along with targeted source files, aggregate-level paths, and a possible safe non-interactive downgrade. This is the primary entry point for the repair policy decision.

Interface defining the output of the repair policy: the chosen action, reason, violation count, targeted source files, aggregate-level paths, unmapped paths, and an optional safe non-interactive downgrade proposal.
Union type of three possible repair actions: 'targeted-repair', 'prompt-full-rebuild', 'require-full-rebuild'. Used by RepairPolicyDecision.
